rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 1, Motion To Dismiss Complaint Against Defendant Brian Bunger. GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PETER ROGOSIN VS. BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 1, DEFENDANT BAY AREA AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTS THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT MAURIELLO PAID PLAINTIFF SHOWING THERE IS NO CAUSATION OR DAMAGE. (UMF 8 & 13) DEFENDANT ALSO PRESENTS EVIDENCE THAT IT PROVIDED A RESPONSE TO EACH PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST. (UMF 14-18.) PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT CITE TO ANY OPPOSING EVIDENCE. (CCP SECTION 437c(b)(3).) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: GEE WAY WONG VS. HUNG CHUN LEUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motiion For Leave To File Second Amended X-Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 2, CROSS-COMPLAINANTS HUNG LEUNG AND ZHUO LAU'S Motiion For Leave To File Second Amended Cross-Complaint. DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANTS DO NOT PROVIDE A SUFFICIENT EXCUSE FOR THE DELAY. THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WERE AVAILABLE IN JULY 2009, AND NO EXPLANATION IS PROVIDED FOR CROSS-COMPLAINANTS DELAYS IN OBTAINING THOSE DOCUMENTS. SUBSTANTIAL PREJUDICE TO CROSS-DEFENDANTS IS DEMONSTRATED. THE TRIAL IS SCHEDULED FOR OCTOBER 18, 2010, ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY WOULD BE NECESSARY TO DEFEND THE ADDITIONAL ALLEGATIONS WHICH SIGNIFICANTLY EXPAND THE SCOPE OF THE LITIGATION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PETER HEIN VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And Request For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And Request For Sanctions. GRANTED. PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO RESPOND TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE) WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO PAY THE MOVING PARTY $1,575 IN SANCTIONS AS THE REASONABLE COSTS AND FEES INCURRED IN OBTAINING THIS ORDER. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE M. HUEY VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 10
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compalling Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Form Interrogatories, And Special Interrogatories; Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFFS JULIE HUEY, GENE WOO, AND IRENE WOO'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Form Interrogatories, And Special Interrogatories; Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Counsel. DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL DID NOT ENGAGE IN A REASONABLE AND GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO MEET AND CONFER. REASONABLE SANCTIONS AWARDED TO DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,870. (CCP SECTION 2023.010(h) AND CCP SECTION 2023.020.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MANOLITA G. ARROYO VS. BOARD OF TRUSTEES, SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS BOARD OF TRUSTEES, SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE, AND CLARA STARR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT BASED ON LACK OF JURISDICTION. FINDINGS MADE IN ARBITRATION OF A GRIEVANCE PURSUANT TO A CBA ARE NOT ENTITLED TO COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL IN THE EMPLOYEE'S LATER LAWSUIT FOR STATUTORY CLAIMS ABSENT (1)A CLEAR AND UNMISTAKABLE WAIVER OF THE EMPLOYEE'S RIGHT TO SUE AND (2)THE ARBITRATION WAS CONDUCTED IN A MANNER THAT ALLOWED FOR A FULL LITIGATION AND FAIR ADJUDICATION OF THE CLAIM. TAYLOR V. LOCKHEED MARTIN CORP. (2003) 113 CAL.APP.4TH 380; CAMARGO V. CALIFORNIA PORTLAND CEMENT CO. (2001) 86 CAL.APP.4TH 995. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (RETALIATION) AND 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (HARASSMENT). TO SUPPORT RETALIATION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SHOW SHE ENGAGED IN A PROTECTED ACTIVITY, DEFENDANT SUBJECTED HER TO AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION, AND A CAUSAL LINK EXISTS BETWEEN THE PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND DEFENDANT'S ACTION. TO DEMONSTRATE HARASSMENT, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE HARASSMENT WAS SEVERE OR PERVASIVE. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ACTIONS OF STARR OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF JOB DUTIES WHICH ARE NOT NECESSARY TO PERSONNEL MANAGEMENT. SEE RENO V. BAIRD (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, BY AND THROUGH, VS. CARL ERNST JR
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 7, 2010, line 7, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO DEFENSES (3)LACHES (SEE NORTHRIDGE HOSP. FOUND. V. PIC N' SAVE NO.9 INC. (1986) 187 CAL.APP.3D 1088); (8)PAYMENT; (10)OFFSET; (19)REPAIR AND DEDUCT; (11)UNJUST ENRICHMENT; (13)FRUSTRATION OF PURPOSE; (16)PORT'S BREACH; (17)ANTICIPATORY REPUDIATION; (18)BREACH OF WARRANTY OF FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE; (14) EXCUSED PERFORMANCE; (15)PREVENTION OF PERFORMANCE; (22)VOID AS AGAINST PUBLIC POLICY; (23)AMBIQUITY OF CONTRACT; AND (24)SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE. MOST OF THESE DEFENSES MAY BE APPLICABLE TO A BREACH OF CONTRACT ACTION, NOT AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER, AND ARE UNRELATED TO THE ISSUE OF POSSESSION. SOME OF THE DEFENSES, SUCH AS CONTRACT IS AMBIGUOUS/ARE NOT COGNIZABLE. MOTION GRANTED AS TO DEFENSES 25 OTHER ACTION PENDING (REYNOLDS DECLARATION, PARAGRAPH 14) AND (29)LEASE NOT TERMINATED (DEFENSE NOT COGNIZABLE.) MOTION DENIED AS TO REMAINING DEFENSES, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. =(302/CWW)
